verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a canonical type II transmembrane glycosyltransferase whose primary and dominant localization is the trans-Golgi apparatus. Its N-terminal cytoplasmic tail and TM domain anchor it to the Golgi membrane, with the catalytic domain facing the Golgi lumen — not the extracellular face of the plasma membrane. A minor PM pool has been documented in specific contexts (e.g., mouse sperm-egg recognition), but this is not established as stable, baseline outer-leaflet surface localization in human cells. The soluble form arises from proteolytic shedding in the stem region and is secreted — it does not remain anchored to the plasma membrane outer leaflet. No therapeutic programs targeting it as a surface-accessible antigen are documented. Overall, the protein body is not accessibly presented on the extracellular face of the plasma membrane under baseline conditions.
